user_query:
I’m preparing for an executive review on the ROI tradeoffs of on‑premise versus cloud. Using the local inputs under input/, please create two artifacts and one helper CSV grounded strictly in those files:

Inputs to use:
- input/onprem_cluster.yaml
- input/cloud_plan.json
- input/service_limits.yaml
- input/past_notes.md

Deliverables:
1) output/exec_brief.md — A concise briefing with these clearly labeled sections:
   - Executive Summary (≤120 words): Frame my skepticism about on‑prem ROI vs cloud while acknowledging where cloud might be leaner. Avoid generic platitudes; reference the totals you computed below.
   - Baseline From Local Configs: Present totals for CPU cores/vCPU and Memory (GiB) for three categories — On‑Prem (from onprem_cluster.yaml), Cloud Plan (from cloud_plan.json), and Service Requests (from service_limits.yaml). Include the numbers directly in the text and make them easy to spot. Also note storage from both plans: on‑prem storage_tb and cloud storage (ssd_gb and standard_gb) as raw values.
   - Observations: 3–5 bullets, including whether the planned cloud capacity is above or below total service requests, and whether on‑prem capacity materially exceeds needs. Call out any right‑sizing opportunities.
   - Quantified On‑Prem Power Cost: Compute implied annual power cost to the nearest dollar using average_power_kw × 8760 × power_cost_usd_per_kwh from onprem_cluster.yaml and include the computed value inline.
   - Risks & Watchouts: At least 3 bullets tied to the inputs (e.g., egress_gb_per_month and backup retention needs if present), not generic risks.
   - Decision Checkpoints: 3 concrete questions the exec team must answer to move forward, grounded in the above.

2) output/meeting_notes_action_items.md — Meeting notes for the upcoming review that include:
   - Attendees: Extract the Participants list from input/past_notes.md.
   - Agenda: ROI tradeoffs (on‑prem vs cloud), resource/limits review, risks, and decisions to make today.
   - Key Data Points: Summarize the CPU/Memory totals and the annual power cost from the brief in one short section.
   - Open Questions: Copy the three Open Questions from input/past_notes.md verbatim.
   - Action Items: For each Open Question, create one action item with an owner assigned by role as follows (ensure owners are drawn from the Participants in past_notes.md):
       • Egress-related item → assign to the Network Engineering Manager.
       • Backup retention item → assign to the Backup Administrator.
       • Test environment item → assign to the SRE Manager.
     Add one additional action item: “Validate reserved coverage assumptions (50% reserved, 12% savings plan) against last quarter workloads” and assign it to the FinOps Lead. For all action items, include a due window formatted exactly as “Due: 7d”. Keep each action item as a single bullet with owner and due window inline.

3) output/resource_summary.csv — A helper CSV with the header: metric,value_onprem,value_cloud,value_services,unit. Include exactly two rows:
   - cpu_cores,<sum of on‑prem cores>,<sum of cloud vCPU>,<sum of service cpu_request>,cores
   - memory_gib,<sum of on‑prem memory GiB>,<sum of cloud memory GiB>,<sum of service mem_request_gib>,GiB

Requirements and notes:
- Read and parse all four input files. Inspect the YAML/JSON configs to compute totals. Do not invent numbers.
- Totals to compute:
   • On‑prem CPU: sum of cpu_cores across nodes in onprem_cluster.yaml.
   • On‑prem Memory: sum of memory_gib across nodes in onprem_cluster.yaml.
   • Cloud CPU: sum over instances of (vcpu × count) in cloud_plan.json.
   • Cloud Memory: sum over instances of (memory_gib × count) in cloud_plan.json.
   • Service Requests: sum cpu_request and mem_request_gib across services in service_limits.yaml.
- Power cost formula: average_power_kw × 8760 × power_cost_usd_per_kwh. Round to the nearest whole dollar and include that value in exec_brief.md.
- Keep the writing crisp and decision‑oriented. Make sure all numbers stated in the documents align with the CSV.

Place the outputs exactly at:
- output/exec_brief.md
- output/meeting_notes_action_items.md
- output/resource_summary.csv All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
